reasoning: The forecast date is June 8, 2025 (less than one month before July 2025). The national average for a gallon of regular is currently around $3.14 (per AAA on June 5). The EIA forecasts summer 2025 regular gasoline to average $3.10, and Brent crude oil to average a relatively low $66/bbl for the year. OPEC+ just announced output increases, and the market generally feels oversupplied. The "All Grades" average is typically only 10-15 cents higher than standard regular gasoline, meaning the national "All Grades" average currently sits around $3.25-$3.30. For July 2025 to average $\geq$ $3.600 across all grades, regular gas prices would need to surge by at least 30-40 cents within a few weeks and sustain that level throughout the entire month of July. Without any immediate geopolitical shock, major hurricane disruption, or refinery catastrophe on the horizon, the chance of such a massive, sustained spike occurring next month is extremely low. I am forecasting the minimum allowable probability of 0.05.
